evidence.scientific_clarity: 10x10 confusion matrix clearly shows per-class accuracy with annotated diagonal and off-diagonal misclassification heatmap; rows=true, columns=predicted labels are correctly oriented.
evidence.typography: Script uses default DejaVu Sans (matplotlib default) with no pdf.fonttype=42 setting; pdffonts confirms Type 3 (non-editable bitmap) fonts embedded in PDF.
evidence.palette: Blues (off-diagonal) and YlOrBr (diagonal) are perceptually reasonable; Blues is largely colorblind-safe, but the dual-cmap split at the diagonal edge creates ambiguity; no explicit colorblind-safety check applied.
evidence.layout_density: Tight layout with colorbar; 10x10 cells well-sized at 7x6 inches; no overlapping labels; sensible tick spacing at fontsize 9.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption correctly describes dual-colormap design and cites per-class extremes; however dual colormap for diagonal vs. off-diagonal is unconventional and may prompt Reviewer 2 to question why the same scale was not used.
evidence.vector_fidelity: figure.pdf exists (26K, PDF 1.4) but pdffonts shows Type 3 fonts; script omits matplotlib.rcParams['pdf.fonttype'] = 42, so text is not selectable/editable.
evidence.file_format: All required files present and non-trivial: figure.pdf (26K), figure.png (57K), figure.svg, caption.tex (1 line), gen_figure.py (69 lines).
evidence.caption_quality: Caption explains row/column semantics, dual colormap usage, and provides two specific numerical claims (C8=92 correct, C1=69 correct), giving a concrete scientific finding.

overall_strengths: Clear and well-annotated confusion matrix with per-cell values, readable diagonal text on colored background, and a descriptive caption with numerical findings.
All required output files are present (PDF, PNG, SVG, caption.tex, gen_figure.py) and tight layout makes efficient use of panel space.
overall_weaknesses: Missing pdf.fonttype=42 (and svg.fonttype) settings in the generation script; PDF embeds Type 3 bitmap fonts instead of editable Type 42, making text non-selectable in vector output.

Default DejaVu Sans font is used without customization; the unconventional dual-colormap approach (different scales for diagonal vs. off-diagonal cells) risks Reviewer 2 pushback and is not explained in the caption.
